extra_model_fields.verifier_issues_summary: Evidence is largely grounded in the available previews (expert_memo.md, agent_trace.json, actual_outputs.json) and the grader's stated downgrade (0.8) is justified by the inability to programmatically inspect the .xlsx. Two issues were found: (1) internal score inconsistency across fields (some fields report 1.0 while final/model score is 0.8), and (2) a claim in the trace that the manual OOXML write "preserves formulas" and thereby preserves formula provenance cannot be verified from previews (openpyxl_unavailable). No hidden-reference leakage detected.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

extra_model_fields.grounding_details: expert_memo.md content is fully previewed and matches grader claims (enumeration, sensitivity table, exception labels). agent_trace.json and actual_outputs.json are parsed and support the narrative that artifacts were created and that openpyxl was unavailable. The .xlsx itself could not be parsed (parse_status=openpyxl_unavailable), so workbook-level formula/cell-provenance claims cannot be confirmed.
recommendations: Normalize/align all score fields (final_score, score, model_score, rubric item scores) so they are consistent and traceable.

Provide either a parsed .xlsx preview (install openpyxl/xlsxreader) or include an exported formulas sheet / machine_readable_formulas.json to demonstrate that cells are formulas (not hard-coded values).

Provide a separate machine_readable_exceptions.json artifact (in addition to the JSON block embedded in the memo) and explicitly state any multipliers/units for fixed_cost_k to remove the noted ambiguity.
unsupported_claims: Trace statement that manual OOXML write preserved formulas and cell-level provenance is unverified by available previews.

Memo uses fixed_cost_k * 1000 in numeric expressions while simultaneously flagging fixed_cost_k scale as needs_review (ambiguous assumption) - this is noted by grader but remains an unresolved assumption.

metadata_json.public_response_summary: Model verifier checked grounding and score consistency.

issues.message: The grader asserts that formulas and row-level totals are mirrored in facility_location_4.xlsx, but programmatic parsing of the XLSX failed in the execution environment (openpyxl unavailable). The expert memo provides the formulas and locations, however the grader appropriately flagged that the workbook-level verification could not be independently confirmed.
issues.recommendation: Either include a machine-parsable plain-text export of critical calculation blocks (or ensure openpyxl/xlsxwriter is available in the grader environment), or embed raw cell formulas as text in the memo so claims about workbook formulas can be independently checked.
issues.title: Workbook content assertions not independently verified

issues.message: The rubric required a separate machine_readable_exceptions.json_or_csv artifact. The submission embedded an exception label in the expert_memo.md but did not provide the separate machine-readable exceptions file; the grader deducted partial credit for this omission.
issues.recommendation: Provide the required machine_readable_exceptions.json_or_csv file in addition to documenting exceptions in the memo to satisfy the rubric and enable downstream automation.
issues.title: Missing required separate machine-readable exceptions artifact

issues.message: The memo uses a negative sign when describing remediation 'capacity_units  (-12%)' which could be misread; the grader called this out and assumed the memo followed the embedded packet semantics (i.e., the remediation applies across records and reduces capacity by 12%). This is not a leak of hidden references but an ambiguity in the submission itself.
issues.recommendation: Clarify remediation direction and scope explicitly in the memo (e.g., 'apply -12% to all records' or 'apply to R1 only') to avoid ambiguity.
issues.title: Ambiguous sign/description in remediation specification noted (clarified but assumed)

extra_model_fields.notes: Grader evidence is largely grounded in the expert_memo.md (text preview available and consistent). The single substantive verification gap is programmatic verification of the XLSX contents due to missing openpyxl in the execution environment; the grader explicitly documents this limitation and adjusts score accordingly. Hidden-reference leakage flags are false across grader outputs.

metadata_json.verifier_notes: The grader's numeric derivations (baseline totals, scenario enumeration, symbolic expected-cost formulas, and the reasoning that only single-option subsets fit under the budget) are supported by the memo text preview. Score differences between deterministic_precheck (artifact-contract pass) and final model-grade (0.75) are explained in the grader's limitations and raw output (missing separate exceptions artifact and inability to parse workbook). No evidence of hidden-reference leakage was found.
